data.title: Fed meeting expected to show hawkish shift with concerns, indicating a potential rise in interest rates.
data.description: The latest Federal Reserve meeting expected to show a more hawkish approach towards near-term interest rates is set to take place today. This shift in tone may suggest a more aggressive stance on interest rates in the near future.

data.title: Iran weakened after Hamas attack on Israel (Oct. 7, 2023). Stocks hit. More on Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: Following Hamas's attack on Israel on October 7, 2023, Iran's strategic position has significantly weakened. The impact of this event has led to a decline in Iran's influence on the global stage, with many experts stating that "Iran has never looked weaker." Additionally, as a result of the attack and escalating tensions in the region, stock markets experienced a downturn. This change in geopolitical dynamics has raised concerns about the future stability of the region and the potential implications for global markets.

data.title: U.S.-China trade deal raises tariffs on Chinese goods to 55%, resolves rare earths issue. China gains other benefits.
data.description: The recent trade agreement between the United States and China involves a significant increase in tariffs on Chinese products to 55%, as disclosed by President Trump. Additionally, the resolution of rare earth elements trade is also part of the deal. While these details have been confirmed, it is still unclear what concessions China will receive in return. The agreement marks a pivotal moment in the ongoing trade disputes between the two countries.

data.title: U.S. may relax chip restrictions in trade talks with China due to rare earth export curbs. - 94 characters
data.description: Trade negotiations between the United States and China have been accelerated due to Beijing's restrictions on rare earth exports. Wall Street analysts believe that as a result, the U.S. may consider easing restrictions on semiconductor chips. This potential trade-off highlights the strategic importance of rare earth materials in the global market.

data.title: Francisco Partners finishes selling iconectiv to Koch. Partnered with Ericsson, it's a leading telecom firm.
data.description: In San Francisco, a recent transaction has been finalized by affiliates of Francisco Partners, a prominent technology investment firm. The sale involves iconectiv, LLC, a prominent player in telecommunications solutions, along with Ericsson (NASDAQ: ERIC), a major provider of mobile communication solutions. The sale was completed in collaboration with Koch Equity Development LLC ("KED"), the primary investment and acquisition entity of K. This development follows an earlier announcement on August 16, 2024.

data.title: Elon Musk sought $97.4 billion to buy ChatGPT's parent company but Mark Zuckerberg did not sign a deal. Vyome reviews Livechain Inc. options and Mercuries Life Insurance explores a potential sale. Surmodics buyout under antitrust scrutiny. Cenovus Energy agreed to acquire MEG Energy. Private equity deals under closer scrutiny for competition harm.
data.description: Elon Musk made a $97.4 billion offer to buy the parent company of ChatGPT, but Mark Zuckerberg and Meta Platforms did not sign a letter of intent. OpenAI is now in a legal battle with Meta Platforms over the failed takeover bid. Vyome is reviewing options for Livechain Inc., which they acquired in 2021, including launching a new business, creating a crypto treasury, merging, or selling it outright. Mercuries Life Insurance in Taiwan is considering a potential sale or stake sale. Peraso Inc., maker of mmWave wireless tech, is exploring strategic options, while Mobix Labs is seeking direct access to the board. GTCR's acquisition of Surmodics Inc. is under antitrust scrutiny by the FTC. Cenovus Energy agreed to purchase MEG Energy. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Zumpano Patricios adds Sebastian Jaramillo as Income Partner to strengthen Real Estate Practice.
data.description: A new addition to Zumpano Patricios in its Miami headquarters is Sebastian Jaramillo, who joins as an Income Partner. Jaramillo, a board-certified real estate specialist with expertise in complex transactions, landlord-tenant disputes, and market analysis, enhances the firm's real estate offerings. With nearly 20 years of legal experience, he bolsters the firm's capabilities in serving clients across its six office locations nationwide.

data.title: World Liberty Financial’s stablecoin USD1 now on Coinbase, marking global accessibility milestone.
data.description: World Liberty Financial, or WLFI, has recently revealed that USD1, the rapidly expanding stablecoin globally, can now be found on Coinbase (NASDAQ: COIN). This move by WLFI to make their stablecoin technology accessible to both institutional and retail users represents a significant achievement, with the availability of USD1 on Coinbase being a crucial milestone in this endeavor. Users of Coinbase will now have the opportunity to utilize USD1 through various platforms including coinbase.com, as well as the Coinbase iOS and Android applications. This development underscores WLFI's commitment to fostering accessibility and convenience for a wide range of users in the digital asset space.

data.title: EU accelerates digital euro plans post US stablecoin law, weighing public blockchain options like Ethereum, Solana. US Genius Act regulates $288B stablecoin market dominated by USD, raising concerns in Europe about euro's competitiveness.
data.description: European officials are ramping up efforts to push forward with a digital euro following the approval of comprehensive legislation in the United States regulating the rapidly expanding stablecoin industry. The recent enactment of the Genius Act in the US has raised concerns in Brussels regarding the euro's future competitiveness against stablecoins pegged to the dollar. The new US law provides a structured framework for the $288 billion stablecoin market, which is currently largely dominated by the dollar. In response to this development, EU authorities are reassessing potential designs for a digital euro, including discussions on whether to utilize a public blockchain platform like Ethereum or Solana instead of confining it to a... (remaining part of the text is not available)

data.title: VanEck files for first JitoSOL ETF tied to Solana, aiming to bridge institutional capital with blockchain in US markets.
data.description: VanEck, a global asset manager, has officially submitted a registration to the SEC for approval of the VanEck JitoSOL exchange-traded fund (ETF). This ETF is significant as it would be the first to introduce a liquid staking token (LST) tied to Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) into the U.S. regulated market. The proposed VanEck JitoSOL ETF aims to track the price of JitoSOL, which is a tokenized form of staked SOL. Investors utilizing JitoSOL have the opportunity to earn on-chain rewards while also maintaining liquidity for trading or deploying assets within decentralized finance (DeFi) platforms. The Jito Foundation highlighted this fund as the inaugural spot Solana ETF backed entirely by a liquid staking token, underscoring its ability to facilitate the connection between institutional capital and blockchain technology. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Inventor creates modified hospital bed with USB charging ports to reduce boredom, fall risks, and injuries.
data.description: In Bradenton, Fla., on August 22, 2025, an inventor shared that they saw a need for hospital beds to have USB charging ports. This idea led to the creation of SMART CONNECT, aimed at reducing boredom, fall risks, and injuries in hospitals.
Intel CEO to meet with Trump for ongoing discussions - CNBC. $INTC

data.title: Dow jumps 900 points as Fed hints at rate cut, hitting all-time high. Nvidia earnings in focus.
data.description: On Friday, the Dow Jones reached a record high amid growing optimism about a potential interest rate reduction at the September Federal Reserve meeting. The stock market surged with the Dow gaining 900 points, reflecting investors' confidence in a rate cut. The positive market activity was also driven by anticipation of Nvidia's earnings report. The news of the Dow's impressive climb was covered live by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Branded Legacy partners with Stanford's Dr. Eran Bendavid to combat opioid crisis with evidence-led innovation.
data.description: Utilizing data-driven approaches to address the worldwide opioid crisis, groundbreaking initiatives are being implemented to combat this pressing issue. These evidence-led innovations aim to tackle the escalating crisis on a global scale. With opioid-related deaths reaching staggering numbers in recent years, the need for effective strategies is more critical than ever. By leveraging evidence-based solutions, organizations are striving to make a significant impact in fighting the opioid epidemic and saving lives. This forward-thinking approach signifies a pivotal shift in addressing the crisis through innovative and data-informed measures.

data.title: EastGroup Properties increases quarterly dividend by 10.7% to $1.55 per share from $1.40 per share.
data.description: In Jackson, Mississippi on August 22, 2025, EastGroup Properties, Inc. disclosed an uptick of 10.7% in its quarterly dividend. This decision, approved by the Board of Directors, elevates the dividend from $1.40 per share to $1.55 per share. Shareholders can anticipate the increased dividend payout in the upcoming period.
